parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative delta adds 6600+ lines including a new retry engine, BigModel error dispatcher, and significant glm-client refactoring — but the most critical integration point (glm-client.mjs, 155 lines changed) has no dedicated test file. Retry and error-classification logic is well-tested in isolation (retry.test.mjs: 352 lines, bigmodel-errors.test.mjs: 189 lines) yet the module that wires them into live HTTP calls is untested. This is exactly the layer where silent misclassification, wrong retry decisions, or auth-header regressions would hide until they hit users. Additionally, tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) and the deleted process.mjs lack migration evidence. Recommend blocking until glm-client receives dedicated integration tests.

findings.title: No dedicated tests for glm-client.mjs despite 155 lines of changes integrating retry and error-dispatch
findings.body: glm-client.mjs is the sole module making HTTP calls to the external API. It absorbed 155 lines of changes to integrate the new retry.mjs engine and bigmodel-errors.mjs error classifier. While those two libraries have strong isolated tests, the integration layer — deciding which errors are retryable, computing backoff, propagating classified errors back to callers — is untested. A misclassified HTTP status code, a missing auth header after retry, or a swallowed network error would only surface at runtime against the live API. The test suite has 14 test files covering other modules; glm-client is conspicuously absent.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/glm-client.test.mjs covering: (1) retry on retryable error codes with correct backoff, (2) non-retryable errors propagate immediately, (3) auth headers survive across retries, (4) response parsing on success/failure, (5) timeout behavior. Mock HTTP calls to avoid external dependency.

findings.title: No dedicated tests for tracked-jobs.mjs despite 78 lines of state-management changes
findings.body: tracked-jobs.mjs manages job lifecycle state and had 78 lines changed. Incorrect state transitions here can cause duplicate job submissions, lost jobs, or stale-state reads. No dedicated test file exists (tests/job-render.test.mjs covers rendering, not job tracking logic). Under concurrent or retried operations, race conditions in job state would be invisible until they corrupt user-visible state.
findings.recommendation: Add tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs covering: job creation, state transitions, idempotent duplicate detection, and concurrent modification scenarios.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted without visible migration test
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines) was deleted entirely. If any runtime path still imports from it — including indirect imports through barrel files or dynamic imports — the failure will be an uncaught ImportError at runtime, not a test failure. No test file explicitly verifies the absence of dangling imports to this module.
findings.recommendation: Verify no remaining import references to process.mjs (grep for 'from.*process' and 'import.*process'). If the check-imports.mjs script already covers this, add an explicit test case that asserts the module is not imported anywhere.

next_steps: Add tests/glm-client.test.mjs with mocked HTTP covering retry integration, error classification propagation, and auth-header persistence — this is the single highest-risk gap.
Add tests/tracked-jobs.test.mjs for job state machine correctness under retry/concurrent scenarios.
Run grep -r 'from.*lib/process' scripts/ to confirm no dangling imports to the deleted module.
Re-run the full test suite after adding the above to confirm no regressions.
